message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="purple" summary="Step 1 implementation complete, awaiting review">
Team, I've completed the initial implementation for Step 1: Navigation + Dashboard Skeleton. Here's what was built:

**New files:**
- `src/routes/dashboard.rs` -- Dashboard route handler with current_page tracking
- `src/routes/gtd_placeholders.rs` -- Placeholder handlers for /inbox, /next-actions, /projects, /waiting-for, /someday-maybe, /review
- `templates/dashboard.html` -- Dashboard page with summary stat placeholders (dl/dt/dd)
- `templates/gtd_nav.html` -- Shared GTD nav partial (nav links + quick capture + sign out)
- `templates/gtd_placeholder.html` -- Reusable placeholder template for all GTD pages
- `tests/api/dashboard.rs` -- 8 integration tests for dashboard route
- `tests/api/gtd_placeholders.rs` -- 7 integration tests for all GTD placeholder pages + auth redirect

**Modified files:**
- `templates/base.html` -- Added `hx-boost="true"` on body element
- `templates/login.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on login form (auth forms need full page reload)
- `templates/register.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on register form
- `templates/todos.html` -- Added `hx-boost="false"` on logout form
- `src/routes/index.rs` -- Authenticated users redirect to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/routes/login.rs` -- Post-login redirects to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/routes/register.rs` -- Post-registration redirects to /dashboard (was /todos)
- `src/startup.rs` -- Added all GTD routes
- `src/routes/mod.rs` -- Exported new modules
- `static/css/main.css` -- Container max-width 40rem->44rem, GTD nav styles, quick capture, dashboard stats, placeholder page styles
- `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` -- Updated for /dashboard redirect, added axe-core a11y scan of dashboard page
- `tests/api/index.rs` -- Updated redirect expectation to /dashboard
- `tests/api/login.rs` -- Updated redirect expectation to /dashboard

**Key decisions:**
1. Header nav with wrapping flexbox, single `<nav aria-label="GTD lists">` landmark
2. `aria-current="page"` with bottom border accent + semibold for active state
3. Quick capture input in header (form posts to /inbox, but POST handler is step 2)
4. `hx-boost="true"` on body for SPA-like navigation between pages
5. Auth forms (login/register/logout) opt out of hx-boost with `hx-boost="false"` so session cookies work properly with full page reloads
6. Dashboard uses `<dl>` for summary stats (semantic label/value pairs)
7. Container max-width bumped from 40rem to 44rem for the wider nav

**Test results:**
- 82 Rust tests pass (was 74 -- +8 new tests)
- 7 Playwright e2e tests pass (was 6 -- +1 new axe-core dashboard a11y test)
- cargo fmt, clippy clean
- SQLx offline data prepared

Please review and provide your feedback. I'm waiting for 9/9 consensus before committing.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, I've reviewed all the key files. Now let me send my review to Luca.

input.content:
Luca, Carson here. HTMX and hypermedia architecture review of your Step 1 implementation:

## APPROVE — clean implementation

The core HTMX patterns are correct. Let me go through each:

**1. `hx-boost="true"` on `<body>` — Correct.**
Line 10 of `base.html`: `<body class="page-layout" hx-boost="true">`. Exactly right. Every link and form in the app is now HTMX-enhanced by default.

**2. `hx-boost="false"` on auth forms — Correct.**
Login, register, and logout forms opt out of boost. Auth flows need full page reloads to properly handle session cookie changes and redirects. Good call.

**3. Nav structure — Correct.**
`gtd_nav.html` uses `<nav aria-label="GTD lists">` with a `<ul>` list of links. `aria-current="page"` is conditionally rendered server-side based on `current_page`. No HTMX attributes on individual links — boost handles navigation. This is exactly the pattern we converged on.

**4. Quick capture form — Correct pattern, one note.**
The form has `method="post" action="/inbox"` with no explicit HTMX attributes. This means boost will intercept the form submission and do a full body swap. That's acceptable for now since the inbox POST handler doesn't exist yet (it's Step 2). When we build US-GTD-1, we'll need to add explicit `hx-post="/inbox"` and `hx-target` to override boost so the capture stays in-place without a page swap. But for this skeleton step, the current markup is correct.

**5. Route structure — Correct.**
All GTD routes are registered in `startup.rs`. The URL structure matches our converged design: `/inbox`, `/next-actions`, `/projects`, `/waiting-for`, `/someday-maybe`, `/review`, `/dashboard`. Each is a GET route with auth guard. Clean.

**6. Placeholder pages — Good approach.**
The reusable `GtdPlaceholderTemplate` with `current_page`, `page_title`, `heading`, and `description` is a clean way to get all routes working with minimal code. Each placeholder renders a full page that extends `base.html`, includes the nav, and shows a heading. Navigation between all pages works end-to-end via boost.

**7. Dashboard with `<dl>` — Correct semantics.**
Definition list for label/value stat pairs is the right semantic choice. The stats are hardcoded to 0 for now, which is fine for the skeleton.

## One Minor Concern

The quick capture label is `visually-hidden`. US-GTD-8 acceptance criteria says "Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text" (from US-GTD-1). For the skeleton this is fine, but when we build the real capture in Step 2, I'd expect Steve Schoger and Heydon to weigh in on whether the label should be visible or if the placeholder + context is sufficient. Not a blocker for this step.

## Verdict

APPROVE from the hypermedia architecture perspective. The HTMX patterns are correct, the route structure matches our design, boost is properly configured with auth opt-outs, and navigation works end-to-end. No changes needed.
input.summary: APPROVE: HTMX patterns correct, boost configured properly, routes match design

content.text:
Review sent to Luca. APPROVE from the hypermedia architecture perspective. Key findings:

- `hx-boost="true"` on `<body>` is correctly implemented
- Auth forms correctly opt out with `hx-boost="false"`
- Nav uses `<nav>` with `aria-current="page"`, no per-element HTMX attributes
- Route structure matches the converged design exactly
- Quick capture form is correctly structured (will need explicit HTMX attributes when the POST handler is built in Step 2)
- One minor note about visually-hidden label on capture input for future steps

Standing by for team consensus on this implementation step.
